fields.title: Call for Proposals: Samvera Virtual Connect 2026

content.value: Samvera Virtual Connect
will take place
Tuesday, May 12 and Wednesday, May 13, 2026
, from:
11:00 AM–2:00 PM EDT
Virtual Connect is a
free, informal opportunity to share work, ideas, and experiences

with current and potential adopters of Samvera technologies. The event takes place entirely online via Zoom, and presentations will be recorded for asynchronous viewing so the broader community can learn from them.
This year’s Virtual Connect will include two days of programming, with presentations grouped into thematic blocks based on topic, audience, and time.
What the Community Wants to See
Every year, feedback from participants highlights the same request:
more real-world stories from repository practitioners.
The community especially values presentations that highlight:
Local repositories and collections
Practical challenges and lessons learned
Innovative tools and integrations
Experiments, pilots, and works in progress
We strongly encourage
repository managers, developers, metadata specialists, and project leads
to share their experiences. Whether you are showcasing a successful project, a work in progress, or something that didn’t go as planned but taught valuable lessons,
your perspective helps the entire community grow.
Presentation Types
Repository Showcase
One of the most popular parts of Virtual Connect is seeing
repositories in action
Whether you're running a full Samvera-based repository such as
Hyku
, building with components like
ServerlessIIIF
, or experimenting with new approaches, we want to see what you’re building.
Repository Showcases are
5–7 minute “show and tell” presentations
highlighting a repository in
production or development
Examples of what you might share:
A unique or innovative repository implementation
A particularly interesting collection
New features or user experience improvements
Integrations with other tools or services
Lessons learned from building or managing a repository
Short, practical demonstrations are especially welcome.
Presentations
You can propose
5, 10, or 20 minute presentations
We welcome submissions from across the
entire Samvera ecosystem
, including topics directly related to Samvera as well as broader work with open repository technologies.
Below are some themes to inspire proposals, but feel free to submit other ideas.
Accessibility and access
How are you making collections more accessible, equitable, or inclusive? What accessibility challenges could benefit from community collaboration?
“New to Samvera” experiences
If your institution recently adopted Samvera technologies, what has the journey looked like so far?
Adaptation and reuse
Have you implemented or adapted something originally developed by another organization in the community?
Challenges and lessons learned
We learn just as much from difficulties as successes. Share experiences with pilot projects, performance tuning, migrations, or integrations that didn’t go exactly as planned.
Integration
How are you connecting your digital repository with other institutional systems or services?
Ways we work together
Ideas for improving collaboration, processes, staffing models, or workflows across teams.
New ideas and pilot projects
Have a new concept or experiment you’d like feedback on? This is a great space to say:
“Hear me out—here’s something we should try and why.”
Breakout Sessions
Breakout sessions are
45-minute virtual roundtables
, scheduled
before or after the main Virtual Connect programming
These sessions are designed for
interactive discussion
rather than formal presentations. Topics can be
very focused or broadly exploratory
, and they provide a valuable opportunity to connect with others across the Samvera Community.
If you propose a breakout session, you will be responsible for:
Facilitating the discussion
Bringing a few guiding questions or prompts to get the conversation started
Submit Your Proposal
We welcome proposals from
across the Samvera community and beyond
, including people who may be
new to Samvera or considering adoption
Proposal deadline:
Friday, April 17, 2026
We look forward to seeing the many ways your repositories, tools, and experiences are helping advance open, community-driven digital repositories.

fields.title: Rob Kaufman is Hyrax and Hyku Technology Coordinator for 2026

content.value: We’re excited to welcome Rob Kaufman to the new role of Technical Coordinator for the Hyrax and Hyku platforms. This role provides essential technical leadership and coordination at a moment when many institutions are facing funding constraints, and reliance on Hyrax and Hyku infrastructure continues to grow.

Rob brings more than 22 years of development experience and a proven leadership track record. He is the former head of Notch8 and prior to this role served as the Hyku Technical Lead. His deep knowledge of Samvera’s ecosystem, combined with long-standing relationships across the community, uniquely positions him to help us strengthen technical continuity today while building sustainable processes for the future.
What Technical Coordination Will Do for Hyku and Hyrax

Hyrax and Hyku are critical infrastructure for many institutions. They support repositories, preservation, access, and discovery services that our organizations depend on every day. At the same time, institutional funding cuts have reduced the capacity of individual teams to carry coordination, maintenance, and strategic alignment on their own.

The Technical Coordinator role is contracted for 20 hours per month and designed to multiply community capacity by providing high-impact coordination, shared visibility, and continuity across projects so that no single institution has to carry this work alone.
Technical Coordinator Priorities & their Value to Hyrax and Hyku Institutions
1. Technical Triage & Support
- 6 hours/month
Value to institutions:
Your teams get expert guidance without hiring additional staff. Questions are answered more quickly, reducing blockers during implementation and upgrades.
2. Maintenance Coordination
- 5 hours/month
Value to institutions:
Proactive coordination helps prevent crisis situations. Shared planning reduces duplicated effort and makes better use of limited developer time across institutions.
3. Community Process & Communication
- 4 hours/month
Value to institutions:
Developers spend less time navigating processes and more time doing productive work. New contributors and staff onboard faster and more confidently.
4. Strategic Planning & Alignment
- 3 hours/month
Value to institutions:
You gain clearer insight into where the community is headed and how to align your local roadmap with shared priorities.
5. Documentation & Reporting
- 2 hours/month
Value to institutions:
Improved visibility into project health supports long-term planning and helps justify continued institutional investment.
What This Role Cannot Do (Given Current Constraints)
This role is scoped at 20 hours per month, and it does not include:
Extensive hands-on development work
Comprehensive documentation or training creation
Detailed accessibility testing
Ongoing community website maintenance beyond critical fixes
Large-scale mentorship programs
These are all areas where the community could grow as funding stabilizes. The current focus is on high-impact coordination work that multiplies the effectiveness of community contributions.
Funding Shared Staffing for our Shared Responsibilities
This role exists because the Samvera Community recognizes that shared infrastructure requires shared staffing.
To sustain this position beyond 2026 and to further expand it to incorporate priorities like training and documentation, everyone who relies on Hyrax and Hyku for critical infrastructure must
contribute to support
this shared work. No single institution can carry this work alone. Shared funding enables shared leadership, reduces burnout, and protects the long-term health of the software we all depend on.
Supporting Samvera is not just an investment in code but in people, coordination, and continuity. We’ll have more information available in the coming weeks about how to support Samvera through
financial contribution and Partnership.

We are grateful to Rob for stepping into this role. Please join us in welcoming Rob as Samvera’s Technical Coordinator, and in committing together to the shared responsibility that makes this work possible.

fields.title: Christine Peterson Consulting Joins the Samvera Community as Its Newest Partner

content.value: The Samvera Community is pleased to announce
Christine Peterson Consulting
as its newest Samvera Service Provider Partner, strengthening the community’s collaborative network of libraries, service providers, and technologists dedicated to open source repository solutions.

Christine Peterson brings more than 30 years of experience in library technology and over a decade working within open source environments. Through her extensive career, she has supported hundreds of academic, public, and special libraries in implementing a wide range of technologies from foundational networking infrastructure to accessibility initiatives and full-scale software deployments.
Christine’s involvement with the Samvera Community began in 2023 through her work with another Samvera Partner,
Amigos Library Services

, where she served as Project Manager for the Amigos Hyku initiative. In that role she contributed to the organization’s successful launch of Hyku as a service offering. Her continued engagement with the community reflects her appreciation for Samvera’s collaborative spirit, mutual support, and shared dedication to open, sustainable solutions.
Now leading her independent practice,
Christine Peterson Consulting
, Christine is focused on projects that make a meaningful impact and foster collaboration across the library technology ecosystem.

“I’ve been continually impressed by the cooperative and innovative nature of the Samvera community,” said Peterson. “Joining as a Partner allows me to continue working alongside dedicated professionals who share a commitment to open collaboration and long-term sustainability.”
The Samvera Community is thrilled to welcome Christine Peterson Consulting as a Partner.
Learn more
about how Samvera Partnership is a critical part of sustainability and shared governance for all the critical infrastructure supported by the Samvera Community.

fields.title: PALNI and PALCI partner with IOI to build Hyku co-investment and decision-making model

content.value: The Private Academic Library Network of Indiana (PALNI) and the Partnership for Academic Library Collaboration and Innovation (PALCI) are pleased to announce a new partnership with Invest in Open Infrastructure (IOI) to strengthen the long-term sustainability of Hyku, the open-source digital repository platform. Through this project, the
Hyku for Consortia

team—along with key stakeholders in the Samvera Hyku Community and IOI—will support the development of a co-investment and community decision-making model designed to ensure the Hyku platform remains robust, community-driven, and financially sustainable.
Read more on the
Hyku for Consortia
blog.

fields.title: LaRita Robinson Receives the 2025 Julie Allinson Award

content.value: On behalf of the Selection Committee, it is my great pleasure to announce that the
recipient of the 2025
Julie Allinson Award
is LaRita Robinson, Senior Software Engineer at Notch8.
LaRita was nominated by Hyku Technical Lead Rob Kaufman, Shana Moore from Notch8, and Nick Steinwachs from Notch8.

Rob's nomination included, “LaRita is one of the community members who is willing to work on the least fun, least glamorous parts of the code. She continually tries to ensure the pieces she touches work for everyone and not just to solve the current problem. Her efforts in the community are often quiet, but can be critical to ensuring that the platforms Samvera support work properly day in and day out.”

Nick’s nomination shared that “LaRita has so much grit and determination, working to fix the most confusing, old parts of code for the greater community benefit. She has worked tirelessly to bring complex, long-term migrations into Hyku over the finish line.”
Shana pointed out how LaRita's “dedication, warmth, and impact embody the spirit of the Julie Allinson Award”:

“LaRita plays a key role in the Samvera x Notch8 office hours, creating a welcoming space for community members to ask questions, share ideas, and navigate challenges. LaRita is generous with her time and knowledge, and she genuinely enjoys solving the hard problems that others might avoid, bringing clarity and progress to complex issues. Most of all, LaRita loves what she does. She chooses to stay rooted in the Samvera ecosystem not out of obligation, but out of genuine passion for the work and the community.”
Congratulations LaRita, and thank you for your outstanding and continuing contributions to the Samvera Community!

fields.title: Samvera Connect 2025 is now a free, online conference and registration is open!

content.value: Samvera Connect 2025
is now a free, online conference, October 28th - 30th

While we had planned to gather in Mexico City, budget cuts and travel restrictions have made it impossible for most of our community members to attend in person. So we're shifting to an online format and inviting everyone to join in.
We have a
full schedule of excellent presenters
ready to go, AND lots of slots are open for anyone in the community to share repository showcases, lightning talks, and working or interest group updates.
I invite everyone in the community to help us make the most of this event:
Register to attend
Samvera Connect 2025, October 28th - 30th, at no cost;
Register for one of three
workshops
(space is limited!);
Participate in the event

by sharing a 7-minute Repository Showcase or Lightning Talk to highlight your work, whether it is a project update, a new initiative, or a challenge you are navigating. You have the option of a live or pre-recorded presentation.
The conference will run for three hours each day in Zoom meeting format for maximum participation.
We hope you can join us for this year’s reimagined Samvera Connect!

fields.title: Samvera Community Welcomes Amigos Library Services as Newest Service Provider Partner

content.value: The Samvera Community is pleased to announce that
Amigos Library Services
(Amigos)
has joined as our newest
Service Provider Partner
, reinforcing their commitment to the continued growth and sustainability of Samvera’s open-source solutions.

Amigos is a 501(c)3 non-profit membership-based organization dedicated to serving libraries through the sharing of collections, services, and opportunities. With nearly 500 members, including public, academic, and special libraries across Texas, Missouri, Arkansas, Oklahoma, New Mexico, Arizona, and Kansas, Amigos is well-positioned to represent the diverse needs of libraries and cultural institutions of diverse sizes and contexts.
Over the past year, Amigos has been an active collaborator within the Samvera Community, particularly in advancing the
Hyku

multi-tenant repository platform. Their contributions include the development of new features for the Hyku community version, such as ETD (Electronic Theses and Dissertations) and OER (Open Educational Resources) work types, flexible metadata options, and the integration of Hyrax 5 and its updated dependencies. Amigos staff have also supported the community through product testing, documentation, and infrastructure and deployment solutions.

As a Samvera Partner, Amigos plans to deepen its engagement by contributing additional code and enhancements, expanding support for testing and documentation, and offering expertise informed by its broad membership base. They also aim to develop a shared Hyku service to help member libraries meet their repository needs while fostering collaborative resource sharing across the region.

“Amigos’ active participation and technical contributions have already strengthened the Samvera ecosystem,” said Heather Greer Klein, Samvera Community Manager. “We are excited to welcome them as a Partner and look forward to their continued leadership in building sustainable, flexible repository solutions.”

In becoming a Partner, Amigos commits to the principles outlined in the Samvera Community’s Bylaws: contributing resources, participating in governance, staying informed on security issues, and helping shape the technical and strategic direction of the community.
The Samvera Community extends a warm welcome to Amigos Library Services to Samvera Partnership.
Learn more about Samvera Partnership
and how it enables collaboration, powers good open source governance, and ensures the sustainability of our critical shared digital repository infrastructure.

fields.title: Samvera Connect 2025: Explore the Full Program & Save with Early Bird Registration

content.value: The
full program for Samvera Connect 2025
is now live! Join us in Mexico City this October for four days of learning, sharing, and connecting with the Samvera community.
The program includes:
A half-day of workshops to kick things off
Two days of plenary sessions featuring participants from across the community
Keynote address from a leading Latin American repository expert (details coming soon)
Repository Showcases and lightning talks highlighting real-world projects
Interest & Working Group roundtables plus dedicated networking time
Tuesday evening reception to relax and connect
Flexible final half-day with unconference sessions, a Partner meeting, and a developer meet-up
Early bird registration is only $200 through September 5.
See the full schedule, review all the details, and
register today at the Samvera Connect 2025 website.
We can’t wait to see you in Mexico City!

fields.title: Deadline extended to July 28th! Call for Proposals for Samvera Connect 2025 Mexico City

content.value: The
Samvera Connect 2025
Program Committee has extended the deadline for our
Call for Proposals for presentations, panels, lightning talks, and posters
to July 28th.
This year’s conference will be held in person from October 20–23, 2025, in Mexico City.

With your participation we can create a rich and inclusive program that reflects the diversity of experience within the Samvera Community—from newcomers exploring adoption to long-time contributors and expert practitioners.
We welcome proposals on all topics related to Samvera Community technologies and the broader ecosystem of open repositories.
Suggested areas include (but are not limited to):
Case studies and implementation stories
Local migrations and integrations
Repository management and outreach
Development workflows and customizations
Accessibility: challenges and solutions
Metadata practices and innovations

We encourage proposals tailored to any segment of our community, including developers, repository managers, sysadmins, metadata librarians, administrators, and all others involved in Samvera-related work.
Proposal formats:
20-minute presentations
40-minute panels, with a moderated discussion between panelists and a Q&A
5-minute lightning talks
Posters
Submission deadline: July 28th, 2025
Submit your proposal on Sched
We look forward to your ideas and to shaping a conference program that informs, inspires, and connects our community.

fields.title: Join Us for Doc Days of Summer: One-Day Community Sprints

content.value: The Documentation Interest Group invites you to participate in the
​"Doc Days of Summer​," a series of one-day virtual sprints focused on improving our community documentation

! Whether you're a seasoned contributor or brand new to ​t​he Samvera Community, and no matter your role, these days are a great opportunity to ​set aside time to collaboratively improve all types of community documentation.
Upcoming sprint dates:
Friday, July 11, 2025
Monday, July 21st, 2025
Monday, August 11th, 2025
Wednesday, August 27th, 2025

If these days don’t work for you, you can volunteer to facilitate a different day! Just ​email me to get your day added to the list. Facilitation just means you'll be working on Samvera documentation for the day and welcome others to join you.
See the
Doc Days wiki page
for details. You can sign up on the wiki, or just join us in the #documentation channel on Slack on each Doc Day.

fields.title: West Virginia University Libraries Joins the Samvera Community as a Partner

content.value: The Samvera Community is delighted to welcome West Virginia University (WVU) Libraries as our newest Community Partner!

WVU Libraries has been a longtime user of Samvera technologies, powering a range of impactful digital collections that preserve and share the cultural heritage of West Virginia and the Appalachian region. Their flagship repository,
West Virginia History OnView
, provides access to more than 54,000 historical photographs. They are also home to the
West Virginia Folklife Collection

, a statewide initiative supported by the National Endowment for the Arts, which documents the state’s traditional artists and cultural communities through photos, audio, video, and written materials.

In addition, WVU Libraries is a key partner in the American Congress Digital Archives Portal—a project developed using Samvera components and supported by over $1.5 million in funding from the National Endowment for the Humanities, Lyrasis, and congressional appropriations. This initiative exemplifies WVU’s leadership in developing scalable, collaborative digital repository solutions.

Over the past year, WVU Libraries undertook a comprehensive review of their digital infrastructure and developed a new plan to ensure the sustainability of their archives and cultural heritage work. As part of this strategy, they’ve partnered with a Samvera Service Provider Partner,
Notch8
, to develop a Hyku repository and made the strategic decision to deepen their engagement with the Samvera Community.

“We have benefitted greatly from the Samvera Community over many years,” said Jessica McMillen, Director of Systems at WVU Libraries. “As we move forward with new development and infrastructure, joining as a Community Partner helps ensure the long-term success of our projects and supports the sustainability of the open-source ecosystem that makes this work possible.”

WVU’s commitment to the community is already in motion: their developer Tracy McCormick was recently named the Hyku Community Developer through the Hyku for Consortia project. In this role, Tracy will be contributing directly to the Hyku codebase to the benefit of all Hyku repositories.

The Samvera Community is thrilled to welcome WVU Libraries to our Partnership, and we look forward to their continued leadership and collaboration. Their involvement enriches our collective efforts to build robust, sustainable, and open digital repository solutions for libraries, archives, and cultural institutions worldwide.
Are you interested in supporting critical infrastructure by becoming a Samvera Community Partner?
Learn more on the Samvera wiki
or contact Heather Greer Klein, heather@samvera.org.

fields.title: Pledge today to support a fast Hyku 7 release - fundraising drive for May 2025

content.value: The Hyku Community is
fundraising to fast-track critical upgrade work
required for platform performance, security, and quality. The goal of this fundraising is to ensure this work is completed by the end of summer 2025.
The goal: Release Hyku 7 by the End of Summer 2025
One of our Samvera Community Partner Service Providers, Notch8, has secured Rob Kaufman to lead this essential upgrade work which includes:
Integrating the latest Hyrax 5.1 into Hyku
Upgrading Ruby and Rails with full dependency resolution
Comprehensive testing and spec development
Rob has the experience to complete this work quickly, so that it is complete in time for community development work this fall.
Why does this work matter?

For both the Hyku and Hyrax communities to enjoy the “cross-pollination” of features and benefits of both, they both need to be on the same version of Rails, and share the same core Hyrax engine version.
This work
sets up both Hyrax and Hyku for improved quality
with fewer bugs
This work is
a required step to address performance issues
Less divergence (and more convergence) of both projects means
higher quality of releases through streamlined testing and a lower  maintenance burden
Timeline & Budget

Estimated completion: 2 months
Total budget: $20,000
Target release: August 2025
Our Pledge Goal: A $2,000 pledge from 10 different institutions by June 1, 2025

If 10 institutions can each pledge $2,000 toward this effort we will reach our goal. Some institutions may be able to contribute more if needed to help institutions who can’t reach the full contribution goal, so
contribution pledges of any amount are welcome
Why Crowdfund?

By distributing the cost across our community, we can move forward quickly without burdening any single institution. Your contribution, regardless of size, helps maintain the collaborative spirit that makes Hyku special.

If we're not able to reach the target budget, this work will still need to be completed, but it will happen more slowly and without the momentum of a dedicated sprint.
How to Pledge
Email
heather@samvera.org
with your pledge amount. Contributions will be invoiced after June 1st.

fields.title: Join us for Samvera Virtual Connect May 6th-7th: Schedule and Free Registration Now Available

fields.title: Join Our May Community Sprints – Everyone’s Welcome!

content.value: We’re excited to invite you to Samvera’s upcoming
Community Sprints

this May! These virtual sprints bring people together to enhance code and documentation. Whether you're a developer, metadata specialist, repository administrator, or in any other role, there’s a place for you to contribute.
Upcoming Sprints
Documentation Sprint

(May 12–16)
Led by the Documentation Working Group, this sprint focuses on improving FAQs, installation guides, functionality descriptions, and more. Whether you’re an experienced writer or new to documentation, your input will help make resources clearer and more accessible.
Sign up for the Documentation Sprint
Development Sprint

(May 19–23)
Join the Product Owners and Tech Leads of Avalon, Hyku, and Hyrax to work on high-priority community features. This is a great opportunity to contribute code, learn from experienced developers, and collaborate on impactful projects.
Sign up for the Development Sprint
Whether you can join for a day or the full week, your participation makes a difference!

fields.title: New Roles to Strengthen the Hyku Community

content.value: The Sustaining the Hyku Repository Platform Steering Group—a collaborative effort led by the Private Academic Library Network of Indiana (PALNI) and the Partnership for Academic Library Collaboration & Innovation (PALCI) in partnership with the Samvera Community—is excited to announce grant support for two recently defined Hyku community positions.
Nic Don Stanton-Roark
has been named Hyku Product Owner, and
Tracy McCormick
is the new Hyku Community Developer. Defined by the Hyku user community as part of the
Sustaining the Hyku Repository Platform

project, these roles are supported by the Institute of Museum and Library Services (IMLS) under grant number LG-256726-OLS-24. They will enhance Hyku’s development and long-term sustainability as an open-source, multi-tenant repository solution.
Read more and get to know Nic Don and Tracy
on the Hyku for Consortia blog.

fields.title: Call for Proposals for Samvera Virtual Connect 2025

content.value: We are pleased to open the
Call for Proposals
for Samvera Virtual Connect 2025!

Samvera Virtual Connect will be held Tuesday, May 6th and Wednesday, May 7th, 2025, from 8:00 AM - 11:00 AM PDT/11:00 AM - 2:00 PM EDT/15:00 - 18:00 UTC. Samvera Virtual Connect is a free, informal opportunity to present on topics of interest to current and potential Samvera technology adopters.
Virtual Connect occurs entirely online via Zoom, with presentations recorded for asynchronous viewing.
Based on feedback from last year, this year Virtual Connect will be in Zoom meeting mode
instead of webinar mode to allow for easier participation from everyone attending!
We're calling for proposals for breakout sessions, presentations, and repository showcases.
NEW: Repository Showcase

The whole community benefits from seeing different repositories in action! Whether you’re on a Samvera platform like Hyku or using a component like ServerlessIIIF, we want to learn more about what makes your implementation or collections special. This is a 5-7 minute repository “show and tell” open to any repository in production or development.
Breakout Session

Breakout sessions are 45 minute virtual roundtables, scheduled before or after the main Virtual Connect meeting. Breakout sessions can be on a very specific or very general topic, and are a great way to discuss issues and topics of interest with others from across the Samvera Community. If you propose a breakout session, you’re agreeing to facilitate a conversation and bring a few questions or ideas to kickstart the discussion.
Presentation
You have the opportunity to propose 5, 10, or 20 minute presentations
. We’re looking for submissions from across the entire Samvera landscape, directly or indirectly related to Samvera or open repositories generally.
Consider the following themes for ideas, but also feel free to submit other types of proposals.
Accessibility and access:
How are you making your collections more accessible, equitable, or inclusive? What accessibility challenges have you encountered that could use a community approach to address?
“New to Samvera” experiences
: If you or your institution started adopting Samvera technologies recently, how has the journey been so far?
Adaptation
: Have you implemented or are you working on implementing something adapted from another organization in the community?
Challenges
: We can all from each other’s failures, pilot projects, performance tuning, and integration challenges
Integration
: How are you integrating the digital repository into your wider institutional community? How have you integrated Samvera technology with other software and services?
Ways we work together
: Ideas about processes, staffing, and other strategies that help teams work together better
Propose new ideas and pilot projects
: “Hear me out, I think we should try this and here’s why…”
This year’s Virtual Connect will have two days of programming, during which we’ll be coordinating presentations into thematic blocks based on topic, audience, and time.
Submit your proposal
Proposals are due Friday, March 28th, 2025.

fields.title: Request for Information: Hyrax/Hyku Training for Developer Cohort

content.value: The Samvera Board of Directors, in partnership with the Sustaining Hyku IMLS grant, is seeking a
Request for Information

from institutions, organizations, and service providers for a developer training program for Hyrax and Hyku development. Both the Samvera Board and the Hyku grant have funds earmarked for a developer training opportunity in 2025.
Statement of Purpose

Our goal for this training is to produce a cohort of developers who are confident in contributing to Hyrax and Hyku. This would require in person training to get up-to-date on the latest releases and current stack for both platforms, and understanding the processes and best practices for contributing to community code.

We expect attendees to be a cohort of ​six to eight developers, of mixed experience, but all familiar with Ruby on Rails concepts. Samvera would pay the training provider directly for training costs and cover catering for breakfasts and lunches, and attendee institutions would pay for travel and lodging and dinner costs.

We have expected this would take the form of 2-3 days of in-person training with peer programming and instruction, to take place in late summer or early fall of 2025. However, we know there are many forms this training could take and we would like to hear about any other format options that could help meet our goal.
Our budget is approximately $20,000 USD, including breakfasts and lunches for attendees and instructors.
To respond please submit this form by March 18th, 2025.

All submissions will be reviewed by the Samvera Board and Sustaining Hyku Steering Committee, who will select one or more submissions for follow-up with a goal of a contract for the training to take place in 2025.
If you have any questions please contact
heather@samvera.org
or schedule time with me during
Community Manager office hours

fields.title: Announcing Avalon Media System 8.0

content.value: The Avalon Media System team is proud to announce Avalon 8!

Avalon 8.0 includes support for Fedora 6, improved UI/UX and browser support in the Ramp media component library, support for Microsoft cloud storage, and a variety of improvements and bug fixes.
With the move to Fedora 6, all Avalon installations will need to migrate from Fedora 4 to Fedora 6. Instructions on migrating Fedora are included in the
Avalon 7.8 to 8.0 upgrade instructions
Major Dependency Updates
Fedora 6
Rails 7.2
Ruby 3.3
Changes in
Ramp 3.3
Progress bar style changes made for improved seeking
State management has been widely refactored
Captions settings for user sessions are now saved to local browser storage
New component added for Expand/collapse buttons in structural metadata component
Fixed a bug where parameters in URLs passed to Ramp caused errors
Multiple bugs fixed for mobile browsers
Updates
Sharepoint and OneDrive can now be
configured as a source in Browse Everything
, allowing users to connect and upload media files directly from Microsoft cloud storage services
Date is no longer a required field in descriptive metadata for items
Subject field now links out to other items with the same subject
Support for progressive download—Avalon can now stream MP3 files directly without transcoding
The location for temporary files created during processing can now be configured
Optimized waveform generation
Improved handling of thumbnail and poster image generation
mediainfo removed as a dependency
Solr queries updated to improve response times
Performance improvements to item edit pages and HLS manifest generation
Documentation on upgrading to 8.0 can be found on the
Upgrading Avalon 7.8 → 8.0
page on the Avalon wiki.
Full release notes and the changelog for 8.0 are available at
https://github.com/ avalonmediasystem/avalon/ releases

fields.title: Avalon Media System Logo
Hyrax Tech Lead mentorship and training opportunity for 2025

content.value: The Samvera Community is seeking a volunteer
Hyrax Technical Lead for 2026
, and we’re excited to offer
mentorship and training for the role in 2025

. This is an excellent opportunity for a developer to gain valuable experience in community leadership, technical decision-making, and open-source contribution, all with the support of a skilled and collaborative team.

If you’re a developer working at an institution that has implemented Hyrax, this role could be a perfect fit for you. It’s ideal for someone who enjoys problem-solving, learning on the go, and contributing to the evolution of technical design and architecture.
Why Consider This Role?
Mentorship & Training
: Work directly with experienced leaders in the Hyrax community.
Leadership Experience
: Develop your skills in making impactful decisions for an active open-source project.
Tailored Expertise Welcome
: Whether your strengths lie in deployments, databases, Ruby programming, or another area, we can adapt the role to suit your expertise.
The Current Team

In 2025, Randall Floyd (Indiana University) will serve as Hyrax Technical Lead, with Indiana University generously supporting him at 40% time and Daniel Pierce contributing 25% of his time to Hyrax maintenance. Together with Nicholas Homenda (Tufts University), the team will provide strong leadership to ensure a smooth transition for the incoming 2026 Tech Lead.
However,
Indiana University will conclude its staffing for Hyrax technical leadership after 2025
, which is why we’re now looking for someone to step into the role for 2026.
Your Opportunity as a “Tech Lead in Training”
To make the transition manageable, we’re offering a
Hyrax Tech Lead mentorship opportunity in 2025
. This role allows you to:

Commit a portion of your time to Hyrax maintenance. The specifics can be arranged with your institution based on your local development schedule, but ideally this would be a percentage of your time during the Maintenance Working Group sprints every two weeks.
Gain hands-on leadership experience under the guidance of Randall and Daniel.
Receive a
free seat
in any developer training offered by the Samvera Community in 2025.

This is your chance to step into an important leadership position with the full backing of the Hyrax Maintenance Working Group and the Hyrax Interest Group, who will support you by sharing responsibilities.
How to Get Involved
If you’re interested in becoming the
Hyrax Tech Lead in Training
please fill out this form by
February 21, 2025
Have questions? Feel free to reach out to me or join us at the
Hyrax Interest Group call on February 12th
to learn more about the role.
Thank you for considering this opportunity to shape the future of Hyrax while growing your skills and impact in the Samvera Community.

fields.title: Hyrax Logo
The Year in Review and a big thank you

content.value: Thank you to everyone in the Samvera Community for your incredible commitment to building and sustaining our work throughout the year. Your activities -- sharing your knowledge in interest groups, leading community initiatives, participating in community sprints, and attending community events - are what keeps this community so vibrant and welcoming. I am truly excited and grateful to help facilitate it all.
Here is a "Year in Review" infographic
(PDF) sharing some of what we accomplished this year. Please feel free to share this with anyone else who should know that your participation is valuable and gets results. You can also
watch the State of Samvera overview and end of year updates from Samvera technologies on YouTube

.

Wishing you a joyful holiday season, a well-earned break, and a new year filled with even more collaboration and mutual support!
Heather Greer Klein, Community Manager

fields.title: 2024 Samvera Year in Review

previews.excerpt: [Registration is now open](https://samveraconnect2023.sched.com/es/1942978658/2022+Samvera+Connect+Registration) for Samvera Connect 2023, to be held October 23rd - 26th at the Philadelphia Marriott Old City.  Samvera Connect is an opportunity to network with others in the Samvera Community, learn what other organizations are doing with Samvera technologies, and work together to support our open source community.

previews.excerpt: It is our great pleasure to announce that the recipient of the 2022 Julie Allinson Award is **tamsin johnson from University of California, Santa Barbara.**   tamsin was jointly nominated by Julie Hardesty (Indiana University), Jessica Hilt (University of California, San Diego), and Chrissy Rissmeyer (University of California, Santa Barbara). In their nomination letter, they nominated tamsin for the award “in recognition of their outstanding technical leadership, mentorship, and tireless efforts in addressing and raising awareness about issues of equity, diversity, and inclusion in our community.”

frontmatter.title: 2022 Julie Allinson Award Recipient

previews.excerpt: We're excited to announce the release of Hyku 4.0. This major release includes many new features such as Cross Site Search, Theming enhancements, and a whole new in-app with ENV defaults configuration process. This is a major release as adopters will need to update their deployment processes to take in to account new ENV vars which more closely align to Hyrax and the removal of config/settings.yml and config/settigns/{env}.yml files.

frontmatter.title: Announcing the release of Hyku 4.0

previews.excerpt: The [Samvera Repository Online Tour](https://samvera.org/samvera-open-source-repository-framework/take-a-tour-of-active-samvera-repositories/) provides an overview of a range of Samvera-based digital repositories and their collections at institutions across the US and UK. Click through to explore how Samvera technologies allow institutions and organizations to organize and provide access to diverse materials -- from oral histories and historic photographs, to student projects and research data.
